hypothesis: If a ∆-learning XGBoost model predicting the difference between GFN1-xTB and DFT band gaps is trained on MOFs using ST-135, RACs, and APRDF descriptors, then it will yield more accurate DFT band gap predictions than a direct XGBoost model and outperform deep-learning baselines (MOFTransformer, CGCNN) because it learns to correct the systematic errors of the baseline semi-empirical predictions.
